channel.title: Protein Design Podcast
channel.description: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research
channel.itunes:author: Eva Dai
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-03-02
item.description: When ruminants shift from a forage-based diet to a high-grain diet, the rumen environment changes dramatically. | A new study published in the Journal of Animal Science and Biotechnology tracked how the rumen papillae—the finger-like projections in the rumen wall that absorb nutrients—respond at the molecular level during this dietary transition. | What they found was striking: the adaptation process isn't a single, uniform response.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: When ruminants shift from a forage-based diet to a high-grain diet, the rumen environment changes dramatically. | A new study published in the Journal of Animal Science and Biotechnology tracked how the rumen papillae—the finger-like projections in the rumen wall that absorb nutrients—respond at the molecular level during this dietary transition. | What they found was striking: the adaptation process isn't a single, uniform response.

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-03-01
item.description: Coral reefs are often celebrated for their colorful fish and intricate structures, but beneath the surface lies an even more complex world: the microbial communities living inside and around corals. | The research team collected coral fragments and seawater samples from 99 reefs across 32 islands in the Pacific Ocean. | By combining this new data with publicly available coral and sponge microbiome datasets, the researchers built what they call the Reef Microbiome Database, or RMD.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: Coral reefs are often celebrated for their colorful fish and intricate structures, but beneath the surface lies an even more complex world: the microbial communities living inside and around corals. | The research team collected coral fragments and seawater samples from 99 reefs across 32 islands in the Pacific Ocean. | By combining this new data with publicly available coral and sponge microbiome datasets, the researchers built what they call the Reef Microbiome Database, or RMD.
